interactions.summary: Jiang answers the Ukraine-Iran connection by laying out a geopolitical model in which Anglo-American sea power must block any Eurasian heartland trade bloc linking Russia, Iran, and China.
The host asks where Venezuela fits into this broader strategic picture.

Jiang says Trump is not driven by eschatology but by an America First imperial restructuring centered on the Western Hemisphere, and that Venezuela functioned both as a trap that inflated Trump's confidence and as leverage in a coming bargain with China over oil and dollar support.
The host asks for the specific leverage Trump thinks he has over China and whether Venezuelan oil is meant to substitute for Iranian oil.
Jiang says the leverage comes from interrupting China's Hormuz-dependent oil supply and then presenting Western Hemisphere oil as the emergency replacement.

claims.claim: Jiang says Anglo-American empire rests on sea-power control and therefore cannot tolerate a unified Eurasian trade bloc that would negate maritime dominance.
claims.temporal_scope: General geopolitical model restated in the 2026-03-11 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang invokes the Mackinder thesis to explain why America historically resists continental integration in Eurasia and seeks to keep Europe and Asia divided through conflict.
Jiang says a Russia-Ukraine victory could enable a Russia-Iran-China trade bloc with enough energy, food, and continental scale to undermine U.S. sea power and crash the dollar order.
claims.temporal_scope: Strategic consequence model proposed in the 2026-03-11 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang argues that America attacked Iran to block the emergence of a Eurasian heartland trade bloc connecting Russia, Iran, and China.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's diagnosis of present-war purpose as of 2026-03-11.

claims.claim: Jiang says Trump's America First worldview does not end empire but recenters it on the Western Hemisphere under a Monroe-Doctrine logic.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's reading of Trump as of 2026-03-11.

claims.claim: Jiang says Venezuela mattered because a fast victory there gave Trump hubris and made advisers' promises of an easy Iran decapitation more believable.
claims.temporal_scope: Causal explanation offered in the 2026-03-11 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang says Trump wants a grand bargain with China in which China keeps buying dollars and thereby helps preserve the dollar's reserve-currency role.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's description of a planned China bargain as of 2026-03-11.

claims.claim: Jiang says China's immediate vulnerability is oil dependence, especially its large reliance on Hormuz-related supply, which lets the United States offer Western Hemisphere oil as replacement leverage.
claims.temporal_scope: Strategic leverage model stated in the 2026-03-11 interview.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang compresses the strategic grammar into a single prohibition: Anglo-American empire cannot allow Eurasia to cohere into a continental trade bloc.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you can never allow a power to emerge in Eurasia
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the key explanatory bridge between the Ukraine theater and the Iran theater.

signature_moments.moment: The dollar order is reduced to a blunt financial image rather than an abstract reserve regime.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: That phrase gives Jiang's monetary argument its sharp populist edge and links geopolitics to an unsustainable domestic financial architecture.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's name for the sea-power doctrine that fears a unified Eurasian heartland capable of bypassing maritime empire.
Not an anti-imperial retreat in Jiang's usage, but a restructuring of empire toward the Western Hemisphere and away from costly Middle East policing.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang's timeline runs from a Russian breakthrough in Ukraine to a Russia-Iran-China bloc and then to an American decision to strike Iran before that continental alignment hardens.
Jiang says Trump will visit China at the end of the month, placing this monetary and oil leverage theory inside an imminent diplomatic timetable.

uncertainty_notes.note: The dollar-collapse, China-bargain, and oil-leverage chain is Jiang's strategic model rather than a source-verified documentary account inside the interview.
